title: Proxy attacks in UK a real and growing concern, says Starmer
description: The prime minister says he is increasingly worried a number of countries are behind attacks in the UK.
content:
The use of proxies by hostile states to carry out attacks in the UK is a "growing concern", the prime minister has said.
Sir Keir Starmer expressed worry over the use of proxies - groups used by states to carry out criminal acts - by "a number of cou... [3525 chars]

title: Sara Cox to replace Scott Mills on BBC Radio 2 breakfast show
description: Cox’s move from teatime slot comes after Mills was sacked over allegations about his personal conduct
content:
Sara Cox will take over as the new host of the BBC Radio 2 breakfast show, replacing Scott Mills after his sacking over allegations about his personal conduct.
The announcement comes as the BBC faces pressure to explain who knew what and when in rela... [1893 chars]

title: D4vd possessed child sexual abuse images, LA murder prosecutors say
description: Police allegedly found images on iCloud account of singer accused of killing 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez
content:
A Los Angeles prosecutor said that the singer D4vd, who was charged this week in the killing of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez, was in possession of a “significant amount of child pornography”.
Police allegedly found the images on the iCloud acc... [4488 chars]

title: No rape took place outside Epsom church, say Surrey Police
description: The force says the woman concerned had injured her head on a night out and made 'a confused report'.
content:
An investigation into a rape outside a church in Epsom has been closed after police concluded "no sexual offence had occurred".
Surrey Police said following its "thorough investigation" into the incident, which sparked protests and public disorder in... [1901 chars]

title: Meet the 19 metre octopus that may have prowled ancient seas
description: Giant octopuses may have ruled the oceans 100 million years ago, according to fossil evidence.
content:
Giant octopuses may have ruled the ancient oceans 100 million years ago, when dinosaurs roamed the land, according to new research.
Some of the earliest octopuses are believed to have been powerful predators equipped with strong arms for grabbing pre... [1742 chars]
